exemplar.title: To what extent is the Victorian conception of immorality condemned in comparison to the critique of the Aesthetic doctrine in Oscar Wildeâs novel The Picture of Dorian Gray?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is the Victorian conception of immorality condemned in comparison to the critique of the Aesthetic doctrine in Oscar Wildeâs novel The Picture of Dorian Gray?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Clear, focused introduction with coherent purpose and effective section signposting
Consistent linkage of character analysis back to the research question
Appropriate use of close-reading methodology supported by credible secondary scholarship
weaknesses: Comparative element not always equally foregrounded throughout the essay
Methodology range is somewhat limited and lacks explicit justification of source selection
Occasional digressions into plot summary reduce sustained methodological focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essay consistently communicates its topic with clarity and maintains a coherent line of inquiry, minor lapsesâsuch as uneven emphasis on the comparative element and a methodology that could more explicitly justify source choiceâprevent it from reaching the top band. The student nevertheless establishes and sustains a clear research focus, employs a suitable range of methods, and orients the reader effectively, meriting a high midâband score of 5.

strengths: Nuanced integration of primary and secondary material demonstrating good contextual and literary knowledge
Generally accurate and appropriate use of advanced literary terminology
Effective synthesis of critical debates to support interpretive claims
weaknesses: Reliance on non-peer-reviewed sources weakens scholarly authority in places
Occasional partial misapplication of terms (e.g., âfoilâ vs. âtragic victimâ)
Some key concepts (e.g., âanti-artistâ) are introduced without sufficient definition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits a strong command of the novelâs themes and the critical conversation, using subject-specific terminology with confidence. Occasional lapsesâchiefly the use of weaker sources and under-explained theoretical termsâprevent this work from achieving the very highest band, but its coherent understanding and largely effective application of knowledge and concepts warrant a score of 5.

strengths: Sustained, well-supported close readings that tie textual evidence back to the research question
Sophisticated organizational strategy (e.g., foil comparisons) that reinforces thematic contrasts
A cohesive, forceful argument culminating in a synthesis of dual condemnations
weaknesses: Limited engagement with counter-arguments or alternative critical perspectives
Some analytical parallels (e.g., Genesis, Narcissus) overextend beyond the text without sufficient grounding
Evaluation of secondary scholarship is uneven, with quotations occasionally presented without full critical interrogation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay draws on good, relevant research and delivers solid analysis that is mostly well supported by both primary and secondary evidence. The argument is logically developed and the conclusion effectively synthesizes the investigationâs findings. Minor shortcomings in critical evaluation and breadth of counter-reading keep the work from the very top band, but its overall depth and relevance justify a strong score of 10.

strengths: Comprehensive structure including title page, contents, clear headings, and bibliography
Consistent formatting choices (font, indentation, citation style) enhance readability
Title and table of contents effectively guide the reader through the argument
weaknesses: Some typographical inconsistencies (italicization, quotation marks) remain
Occasional overly dense paragraphs hinder flow and would benefit from subdivision
Page numbering is inconsistently applied and not visible in all sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets all key presentation requirements and applies layout and formatting conventions competently. Minor lapses in typographical consistency and paragraphing prevent a flawless execution, placing the work at the upper end of the competent band with a score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay shows an identifiable methodology: close literary analysis supported by secondary scholarship, a contents page, and Harvard-style referencing.  Sources are largely journal articles and monographs of reasonable credibility, indicating some informed selection.  Nonetheless, the range is limited (c. 12 secondary works, several undergraduate papers, and heavy reliance on a few key critics), and there is no explicit explanation of how sources were chosen or how the analytical approach was shaped.  Consequently, the methodology is "mostly complete" but does not demonstrate the breadth or reflexivity expected for the top band.

Formatting conventionsâtitle page, consistent font, citation style, and bibliographyâare largely observed, and in-text citations correspond to list entries.  The word count is supplied and below 4,000 words.  Nevertheless, there are minor inconsistencies: occasional typographical errors, irregular use of italics/quotation marks, and some web links lacking full publication details.  Page numbers are referenced in the contents table but actual page numbering is not visible in the extract provided.  These small issues place the work in the competent 3-4 band with a score of 3 rather than 4.

The research base is **generally sound**: the essay draws on a mix of primary text analysis and secondary criticism to underpin its points.  Most sources are relevant to the RQ, and citations are correctly attributed.  Nonetheless, some references (e.g., blog-style web pages, undergraduate journals) weaken overall scholarly robustness, and there is limited use of more recent or seminal peer-reviewed studies.  The research is thus âgoodâ and mostly relevant, aligning with the upper part of the 4-6 band rather than reaching the sophistication and breadth required for 7-9.

The student establishes the topic early by naming both the Victorian moral code and Aesthetic doctrine and explains, in clear prose, that the essay will weigh Wildeâs condemnation of each in The Picture of Dorian Gray.  The introduction and section headings reflect a coherent purpose and keep the reader oriented throughout.  Minor issues remain: the essay occasionally digresses into lengthy plot summary and scholarly background, and the comparative element ("in comparison to the critique of the Aesthetic doctrine") is not always foregrounded with equal weight.  Nonetheless, the topic is communicated with sufficient clarity and an evident line of inquiry, meriting a solid placement in the 3â4 band at the upper end.

A coherent line of discussion runs through the essay, culminating in a conclusion that synthesises findings about dual condemnations of Victorian morality and Aesthetic excess.  The studentâs argument is generally logical, yet evaluation of differing critical viewpoints is **limited**; alternative readings are noted but not fully interrogated.  Some sections verge on repetition, and the âextentâ element of the RQ is resolved only broadly rather than with nuanced weighing.  The discussion thus demonstrates âadequate to goodâ critical engagement, earning a mid-band score of 4.

The student offers **sustained close readings** (e.g., analysis of Lord Henryâs speeches, the portraitâs symbolism, and biblical parallels) and usually ties these readings back to the central argument.  Claims are mostly supported by textual evidence and secondary scholarship, and the essay moves beyond simple description to interpretative commentary.  However, some assertions (e.g., extended analogies with Genesis and Narcissus) rely more on thematic resemblance than rigorous argument, and counter-arguments are rarely explored.  This places the analysis comfortably in the âgoodâ 4-6 band, meriting a 5 rather than the highest mark.

The essay includes all required structural components: title page with word count, abstract/intro, clear section headings, conclusion, bibliography, and a functional table of contents with page references.  Transitions between sections are generally smooth, though occasional digressions and uneven paragraph lengths affect overall coherence.  Minor lapses (e.g., overly long quotations without indentation, some headings crowded with ellipses) prevent a perfect execution, but the structure is clearly appropriate, situating the work in the 3-4 band at a solid 3.

The student displays **good literary and contextual knowledge**: nuanced summaries of Aestheticism, Victorian morality, and key critical debates support the argument.  Primary textual references are apt and generally well integrated, and secondary sources are used to extend points (e.g., Duggan, Kidd, Pudney).  At times, however, quotations from criticism are cited without critical engagement, and a few sources (e.g., undergraduate theses, web compilations) carry limited academic weight.  Overall understanding is clear and generally coherent, fulfilling the descriptors for the 3-4 band at the upper level.

Literary and theoretical terms such as "Hedonism," "Aestheticism," "synecdoche," and "hamartia" are used with reasonable accuracy, demonstrating subject familiarity.  Occasional misuse (e.g., calling Hallward a "foil" yet treating him as a tragic victim rather than structural contrast) and some over-extended classical comparisons reveal partial misapplication.  The command of terminology is therefore mostly accurate but not entirely consistent, warranting the mid-range score in the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly stated and revisited in the body and conclusion, showing awareness of focus.  However, it is rather complexâcombining two large constructs (Victorian immorality and Aesthetic doctrine) without specifying precise parametersâso at times the discussion drifts toward general thematic commentary rather than tightly addressing âto what extentâ each is condemned.  The connection between subsidiary arguments and the RQ is usually evident but not always sustained, placing the work securely in the 3-4 band but closer to the lower end.

annotations.comment: Overall layoutâincluding consistent font and indentationâenhances readability and aligns with academic presentation standards.
The foil structure comparing Basil to Adam alongside Henry and Dorian is a sophisticated organizational strategy that highlights thematic contrasts.
This section introduces the art-reality distinction but does not explicitly link back to the comparative element of the research question; add a sentence to make this connection clear.
Including the word count at the start meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to administrative conventions.
Several quotations are presented with minimal analysis; deepen the discussion by critically engaging with each scholarâs perspective and drawing explicit connections to your argument.
The Genesis parallel is compelling but would benefit from acknowledgement of Eveâs agency or alternative readings to deepen critical evaluation.
The paragraph is overly long and dense; consider dividing it into two or three shorter paragraphs to improve flow and reader engagement.
The evaluative statement linking Hedonistic excess to inherent immorality demonstrates effective analytical depth and consistently ties evidence back to the research question.
The research question is stated with precision and immediately orients the reader to the investigationâs scope, providing a clear framework for all subsequent analysis.
The title is concise yet descriptive, effectively communicating both elements of the comparative inquiry and maintaining reader interest from the outset.
The introduction provides extensive summary but lacks explicit signposting to show how each section will address the research question; add a brief roadmap.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly linked to the structure of the essay, aiding navigation and reinforcing coherence.
The contents table references page numbers, but no visible page numbering appears on the pages themselves; add page numbers to align with the TOC.
This opening paragraph is very dense; consider breaking it into shorter paragraphs for improved readability and clearer thematic transitions.
The section heading clearly identifies the focus on Victorian morality, providing a strong structural signpost for the reader.
The student integrates Lord Henryâs monologues with apt textual citations, demonstrating solid knowledge of the primary text and its nuances.
Reliance on non-peer-reviewed sources (e.g., web pages and undergraduate journals) weakens scholarly authority; integrate more seminal, peer-reviewed criticism.
The analysis of Alan Campbellâs predicament effectively ties character actions to broader Victorian norms, reinforcing the essayâs methodological focus on close character reading.
Accurate application of literary terms such as âsimileâ and âmetaphorâ demonstrates good command of subject-specific terminology.
The inference of sexual intimacy from âinseparable intimacyâ is interesting but under-substantiated; strengthen this reading with additional textual or historical context.
The subheading could be more precise (e.g., referencing aristocratic hypocrisy) to better reflect the analytical focus of this paragraph.
The classical allusion to Narcissus is insightful but overextended; ensure each parallel is directly grounded in specific passages from Wildeâs text.
Please verify that all classical allusions (e.g., Mandlebaumâs cites) follow consistent citation format with page numbers and publisher details.
The conclusion provides a cohesive synthesis of the dual condemnations and reinforces the nuanced thesis effectively.
The term âanti-artistâ is used without definition; provide a clear explanation of its theoretical origins and its relevance to Dorianâs portrayal.
The argument would be strengthened by addressing a potential counter-reading that regards Aestheticismâs pursuit of pleasure as a form of liberation rather than solely as immorality.
This paragraph on Sybil Vane is very long; divide it to differentiate between summary, analysis, and critical evaluation.
The causal link between Sybilâs artistic âprisonâ and her suicide needs further textual evidence; consider adding a direct quotation that underscores this transformation.
The Genesis parallel for Lord Henry deepens thematic resonance, effectively framing his corrupting influence as a modern Edenic temptation.
Ensure all scriptural citations (e.g., Gen. 3.5, 3.13) follow a consistent formatting style, including parentheses and italics where required.
The bibliography demonstrates a broad engagement with primary and secondary materials, supporting the essayâs scholarly range.
Expand on how epithets like âsickly aimsâ critique the moral underpinnings of Victorian norms, rather than focusing only on their contrast to Hedonism.
The conclusion would benefit from explicitly restating how each major section directly addressed the comparative element of the research question.
The biblical allegory risks overshadowing unique novel dynamics; balance this by incorporating more direct textual analysis of Basilâs dialogue and actions.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (publisher information, access dates, URL styles); apply a uniform citation style throughout.
